content.text:
Acronyms:
- CHIME — Canadian Hydrogen Intensity Mapping Experiment
- DSA-110 — Deep Synoptic Array (110-antenna prototype)
- FLITS — Fitting Likelihoods In Time-Frequency Spectra
- FRB — Fast Radio Burst
- MCP — Model Context Protocol

Six-root workspace; science cluster dominates.

```mermaid
graph TD
  Faber2026[Faber2026 manuscript] -->|pipeline submodule| FLITS[dsa110-FLITS]
  FLITS -->|figures/tables| Faber2026
  Data[iCloud / iacobus CHIME_DSA_Codetections] --> FLITS
  Staging[chime-dsa-documents-area-staging] -.->|stale scratch clone| FLITS
  Holding[research-holding/chime_dsa_codetections] -.->|docs only| Data
```

| Root | Role | Git |
|------|------|-----|
| `overleaf/Faber2026` | AASTeX631 manuscript — 12 co-detected FRBs, DM/scattering budgets | `main` clean; dirty `pipeline/` submodule @ `3a9f69c` (~182 commits behind canonical FLITS) |
| `repos/.../dsa110-FLITS` | Canonical analysis engine (scattering, scintillation, batch, galaxies) | `main`, behind origin 6; heavily dirty |
| `scratch/.../chime-dsa-documents-area-staging` | Stale FLITS clone @ consolidation merge; ~313 deletions — not runnable | frozen `569adc7` |
| `research-holding/.../chime_dsa_codetections` | Consolidation docs only (`CONSOLIDATION_PLAN.md`, `AGENTS.md`) — monorepo lives in iCloud | not a git repo |
| `projects/gcloud-kia` | Dialogflow CX MCP/CLI for HPC knowledge (`my-know-it-all`) | `main` clean |
| `scratch/.../drwatson-py` | Tiny DrWatson.jl-style Python helper (paths, sweeps, tagging) | `main` clean, initial commit |

**Faber2026** — paper shell. `main.tex` + `sections/` (intro → conclusions + association-card appendix). Figures at root `figures/`; pipeline runs in submodule, exports here. Overleaf compiles root only; GitHub Actions rsyncs on push. Active prose: per-sightline α adjudication "in progress"; draft branches `draft/fork-b-finish`, `draft/manuscript-completion`.

**dsa110-FLITS** — where science runs. Kernel: `scattering/scat_analysis/burstfit.py`. Conda env `flits`. Active campaign: `analysis/scattering-refit-2026-06/` (all-exp joint ladder, baseband recovery, burst energies). 8 open deferred items in `.agents/deferred-tasks.md` — all `@human`/`@decision`, gated on citable-α lock (#4). Agent Stop gates: figure-review, deferred-task, protected-branch commit guard.

**Data topology** — code in FLITS; bulk data on `iacobus` → iCloud `Research/CHIME_DSA_Codetections/`; raw on h23. Staging scratch holds `DATA_LOCATIONS.md` + `codetections_manifest.yaml` for migration work.

**Peripheral** — `gcloud-kia` and `drwatson-py` are unrelated to the FRB paper; small, clean, standalone.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>

First order of business: consolidation.

Documents relevant to this project are spread across various directories, not only on this machine but also on various remote machines & servers, including:
- `hpcc` (Caltech Resnick HPC)
- `h17` (OVRO compute server)
- `arc` / `CANFAR` (CHIME/FRB compute server)
- `h23` (OVRO compute server)
- `dsacamera` (OVRO compute server)
- `iacobus` (my backup/old Intel Macbook Pro)

Remind yourself of how to access these machines and where the relevant data and files on each reside.
</user_query>

Gathering access methods and data locations from consolidation docs and machine configs.

[REDACTED]
[REDACTED]
[REDACTED]
[REDACTED]
[REDACTED]
[REDACTED]
[REDACTED]

Acronyms:
- CANFAR — Canadian Advanced Network For Astronomical Research
- CADC — Canadian Astronomy Data Centre
- DSA-110 — Deep Synoptic Array (110-antenna prototype)
- FLITS — Fitting Likelihoods In Time-Frequency Spectra
- HPCC — Caltech High Performance Computing Center
- OVRO — Owens Valley Radio Observatory

**Two-home target:** code → `dsa110-FLITS`; processed data → iCloud `Research/CHIME_DSA_Codetections/` (bulk uploader: **iacobus**). Raw/cold upstream stays on **h23** and **arc**.

---

### Access cheat sheet

| Alias | How | User |
|-------|-----|------|
| `iacobus` | Tailscale `100.93.229.114` (`iacobus-bkp-mbp.taile1537d.ts.net`) | `iacobus` · key `~/.ssh/iacobus_key` |
| `h17` | Tailscale `100.85.172.12` (`lxd110h17-hpc.taile1537d.ts.net`) direct | `ubuntu` |
| `h23` | `lxd110h23.pro.pvt` via `ProxyJump dsa110maas` → `ovro` | `ubuntu` |
| `dsacamera` | `dsacamera.ovro.pvt` via `ProxyJump ovro` | `jfaber` or `dsacamera-ubuntu` |
| `hpcc` | `login.hpc.caltech.edu` via `ProxyJump campus-pi` (Tailscale `100.85.176.43`) | `jfaber` |
| `ovro` | `ssh.ovro.caltech.edu` | `jfaber` |
| `tubular` → `frb-analysis` | CHIME network jump chain | `jfaber` |
| **arc / CANFAR** | No SSH alias — **CANFAR Science Platform** web shell, or **`vos`/`vcp`** with CADC proxy `~/.ssl/cadcproxy.pem` (`cadc-get-cert -u jfaber`) | VOSpace `arc:home/jfaber` |

**h23 via iacobus** (when Mac→h23 banner times out): `ssh -A iacobus 'ssh h23 echo ok'`

**h23→iacobus bulk pull:** `h23_resilient_transfer.sh` — rsync runs *on* iacobus with agent forwarding; data never hits jakob-mbp disk.

---

### Per-machine: what's there

**jakob-mbp (this Mac)**
- Code: `~/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS/`
- Manuscript: `~/Developer/overleaf/Faber2026/` (`pipeline/` submodule → FLITS)
- Data: iCloud placeholders only — `~/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Research/CHIME_DSA_Codetections/`
- Consolidation docs (non-git): `~/Developer/research-holding/caltech/ovro/dsa110/chime_dsa_codetections/`
- Legacy Documents clone (drain, don't develop): iCloud `Documents/Areas/research-holding/.../dsa110-FLITS/`
- Local `.npy` replica (optional dev): `~/Developer/dsa110-local-data/DSA_bursts/` (from arc)

**iacobus** — **processed-data staging + iCloud uploader**
- `~/Research/CHIME_DSA_Codetections/` — ~218 GiB live (8 subdirs; see manifest)
- CloudDocs mirror: `~/Library/Mobile Documents/.../Research/CHIME_DSA_Codetections/`
- Dropbox exit staging: `~/Library/Mobile Documents/.../Dropbox-Migration/` (filterbanks, Faber2025, CANFAR_backup, etc.)
- `codetections_manifest.yaml` — per-subdir provenance + SHA sentinels
- rclone Dropbox API pull already done (24 burst pickles verified)

**h23** — **cold upstream (do not delete)**
- `h23:/media/ubuntu/ssd/jfaber/chime_dsa_codetections/` — organized analysis tree (`data/`, `scattering/`, `dm_budget/`, `localizations/`, …)
- `h23:/media/ubuntu/ssd/jfaber/burst_npys/` — broader DSA `.npy` sample (21 GB)
- `h23:/media/ubuntu/ssd/jfaber/OLD_CHIME_DSA_Codetections/` — historical archive (~79 GB)
- `h23:/media/ubuntu/ssd/jfaber/burstprop_paper/` — earlier paper assets
- `h23:/media/ubuntu/ssd/jfaber/DM_phase/`
- `h23:/dataz/dsa110/T3/` — raw T3 pipeline (**59 TB**, never copy)
- `~/jfaber/nihari/` — **out of scope** (separate project)

**h17** — **active compute for FLITS + CHIME baseband docker**
- FLITS clone: `/home/ubuntu/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS`
- CHIME codetection tree: `/data/research/astrophysics/frbs/chime-dsa-codetections/`
  - wrapper: `bin/baseband_analysis_python.sh`
  - singlebeam `.h5`: `chime_singlebeam/`
- Scratch/products: `/data/jfaber/upchan_codetections/`, `/data/jfaber/chime_singlebeam/`
- Arc archive (Jun 2026 migration): `/data/jfaber/arc_archive_2026-06/`, `/data/Faber2023_bursts/`
- Docker: `chimefrb/baseband-analysis:latest` (CANFAR snapshot; no SP dependency on h17)
- Prior audit: **no** legacy codetection monorepo; imaging/ops focus — but **now** hosts active refit work

**arc / CANFAR** — **canonical `.npy` dynamic spectra + baseband morphologies**
- Live tree (keep): `/arc/home/jfaber/baseband_morphologies/chime_dsa_codetections/`
  - `data/DSA_bursts/` — 12 DSA `.npy` (~250 MB each)
  - `data/CHIME_bursts/dmphase/` — 12 CHIME `.npy`
- Also on arc: `OLD_CHIME_DSA_Codetections/` (~77.6 GB — audit before cleanup)
- Burst configs hardcode these paths; batch runner overrides via `$DATA_DIR`
- Backup copy on iacobus: `Dropbox-Migration/Apps/CANFAR_backup/` (96 MB)
- iCloud archive: `Archives/CHIME_canfar/baseband_morphologies/`
- Access: Science Platform container (native FS for space/brace filenames vos can't handle) or `vcp`/`vls` with proxy cert

**hpcc** — **batch joint-fit Slurm runs**
- Repo: `/home/jfaber/flits/dsa110-FLITS` (venv `/home/jfaber/flits/venv/`)
- Scratch: `/central/scratch/jfaber/flits-runs/` (`data/joint/`, `logs/`)
- Monitor: `analysis/scattering-refit-2026-06/hpcc/monitor.sh`
- Example result: `zach_joint_fit_C2D3_pbf-exp-exp.json`

**dsacamera** — **negligible codetection content**
- Imaging/HI/meridian ops; quick survey found no analysis trees under `/home/jfaber`
- Stale code snapshots (future quarantine): `/data/jfaber/misc/FLITS/`, `backup-dsa110-scat/`
- `flits` conda env may exist without project dir — verify before assuming relevance

---

### iacobus data layout (consolidated ~189 GiB)

| Subdir | Source | Contents |
|--------|--------|----------|
| `burst_pickles/` | Dropbox | 24 full-Stokes interp `.pkl` (~61 GB) |
| `burst_npys/` | h23 | 52 DSA waterfalls (~21 GB) |
| `dsa_fullstokes_waterfalls/` | h23 | 58 IQUV `.npy` (~6 GB) |
| `scattering_results/` | h23 | PDFs, corners, fits (~86 MB) |
| `dm_budget/` | h23 + Mac | MCMC / nested repos (~456 MB) |
| `metadata/` | h23 + Mac | CSVs, localizations, `interveners.ipynb` |
| `presentations/` | Mac | DM phase deck |
| `archive/` | h23 | `OLD_CHIME_DSA_Codetections/` + `burstprop_paper/` (~101 GB) |

Long-term canonical path: iCloud `Research/CHIME_DSA_Codetections/` — jakob-mbp sees placeholders; bytes materialize on demand.

---

### Consolidation status (Apr 2026 plan)

- **Done:** h23→iacobus rsyncs, Dropbox→iacobus rclone, satellite fold into FLITS (`consolidation-2026-04` tag), quarantine moves (`FLITS/`, `FRB/`, `old_stuff/`), manifest written
- **In flight / deferred:** iacobus→iCloud `bird` upload; reconcile `_staged` vs canonical CloudDocs tree; dsacamera remote clone snapshots; Dropbox gate 4 (cancel after insurance window)
- **Explicitly out of scope:** `nihari/`, `Faber2024_bursts/`, h23 raw T3 (59 TB)

---

### Key doc oracles

- `dsa110-FLITS/DATA_LOCATIONS.md` + `DATA_SOURCES.md` + `codetections_manifest.yaml`
- `chime_dsa_codetections/CONSOLIDATION_PLAN.md` + `PROJECT_INVENTORY.md` (research-holding copy)
- `dotfiles/memory/project_dsa110-flits-scattering-budget.md` (arc quota + Jun 2026 arc→h17 migration)

<user_query>

How is arc/CANFAR accessed?
</user_query>

**No SSH alias.** Two paths:

**1. CLI (from jakob-mbp)** — `vos`/`vcp`/`vls`/`vrm` in conda `py312` (`~/.local/bin`)

```bash
# refresh cert (~monthly; needs password in TTY)
cadc-get-cert -u jfaber --days-valid 30
# cert lands at ~/.ssl/cadcproxy.pem

vls arc:home/jfaber
vls arc:home/jfaber/baseband_morphologies/chime_dsa_codetections/data/DSA_bursts
vcp arc:home/jfaber/... local/path
```

VOSpace root: `arc:home/jfaber` ≡ `/arc/home/jfaber/` on Science Platform.

**2. CANFAR Science Platform** — web shell / notebook container where `/arc` is mounted natively. Needed for filenames with spaces/braces that `vos` CLI rejects.

---

**Burst data canonical path**
```
/arc/home/jfaber/baseband_morphologies/chime_dsa_codetections/data/
  DSA_bursts/          # 12 DSA .npy
  CHIME_bursts/dmphase/ # 12 CHIME .npy
```

**Pull to another host:** `vcp` arc → h17 (Jun 2026 arc cleanup did this). h17 does **not** mount `/arc` — files must be copied in.

**CHIME network SSH** (`tubular` → `frb-analysis`) is separate — McGill compute, not arc storage.

**Cert status (probed):** valid until **Jul 18 2026**.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>

what is "vls"?
</user_query>

**`vls`** — list command from the **`vos`** Python package (CADC VOSpace client). Like `ls`, but for cloud storage at `arc:home/jfaber/...` instead of local disk.

```bash
conda activate py312   # or conda run -n py312
vls arc:home/jfaber
vls -l arc:home/jfaber/baseband_morphologies/chime_dsa_codetections/data/DSA_bursts
```

Uses `~/.ssl/cadcproxy.pem` for auth. Sibling commands: **`vcp`** (copy), **`vrm`** (delete), **`vtag`** (metadata).

[REDACTED]

<user_query>

Does this work similar ot ssh or does it not let us run anything on arc?
</user_query>

**Not like SSH.** `vls`/`vcp`/`vos` are **storage-only** — list, copy, delete files on arc. No shell, no `python`, no job runner.

| | `ssh h17` | `vls`/`vcp` |
|---|---|---|
| What it does | Remote shell + run commands | Remote filesystem I/O |
| Analogy | SSH | `scp` / S3 CLI |

**To run code on arc/CANFAR:** CANFAR **Science Platform** — web session launches a container with `/arc/home/jfaber/` mounted; you get a terminal/notebook there. That's where pipeline scripts expect `$DATA_DIR=/arc/home/jfaber/...` to live.

**Typical split:**
- **Mac:** `vcp` pull/push files via cert
- **Science Platform or h17/h23:** run fits/analysis (h17 copies arc data locally because it can't mount `/arc`)

[REDACTED]

<user_query>

But this cannot be done via the CLI?
</user_query>
[REDACTED]

Correct — **`vls`/`vcp`/`vos` cannot run code on arc.** Storage only.

**CANFAR compute** in your setup = **Science Platform in a browser** (container + `/arc` mounted + terminal/notebook). No `ssh arc` alias; not part of the `vos` toolchain.

**Headless CLI workflow you actually use:**

```
vcp  arc → local/h17     # move files
ssh h17 / hpcc          # run fits there
```

h17 even ships the CANFAR `baseband-analysis` **docker image** locally so you don't need a live Science Platform session for that pipeline.

**Bottom line:** arc via CLI = file transfer only. Execution = Science Platform (browser) or copy off arc and run on h17/hpcc/Mac.

content.text:
Not impossible — two different CLIs:

| CLI | Package | Does |
|-----|---------|------|
| **`vos`** (`vls`, `vcp`, …) | already in `py312` | **Storage only** — list/copy arc files |
| **`canfar`** | `pip install canfar` — **not installed here** | **Science Platform** — launch sessions, run jobs |

**`canfar` examples:**
```bash
canfar auth login          # reuses ~/.ssl/cadcproxy.pem if valid
canfar create notebook skaha/astroml:latest
canfar create headless skaha/terminal:1.1.2 -- python /arc/home/jfaber/...
canfar ps / canfar logs / canfar delete
```

Headless mode = run a command inside a container with `/arc` mounted — closest thing to "SSH to CANFAR" from terminal. Still not a persistent shell alias; it's session/job based.

**Your machine today:** `vos` ✓ · `canfar` ✗ · practical fallback = `vcp` + `ssh h17`/`hpcc`.

## CADC arc storage (VOSpace `arc:home/jfaber`)

- **Storage CLI (`vos`):** `vcp`/`vls`/`vrm`/`vtag` from `vos` 3.6.4 with X509 proxy cert `~/.ssl/cadcproxy.pem` (CADC user `jfaber`; refresh `cadc-get-cert -u jfaber --days-valid 30`, password via TTY only — Jakob runs it; **current cert exp Jul 18 2026**). Headless run: `canfar create headless skaha/terminal:1.1.2 -- python /arc/home/jfaber/...`. CHIME image in use: `images.canfar.net/chimefrb-public/baseband-analysis:v1.9.0`. **Two CLIs:** `vos` = file I/O only; `canfar` = launch sessions / run jobs with `/arc` mounted.

<user_query>

So what exactly are we able to use canfar for given the cli?
</user_query>

**Two layers — don't mix them up:**

| Tool | What it does |
|------|----------------|
| `vls` / `vcp` / `vos` | **Files on arc** — list, copy, delete |
| `canfar` | **Compute on CANFAR** — launch containers with `/arc` mounted, run code |

---

**What `canfar` can do**

**Sessions**
- `canfar create notebook <image>` — Jupyter with `/arc/home/jfaber/` inside
- `canfar create headless <image> -- <cmd>` — run a script, exit when done
- `canfar ps` / `info` / `logs` / `events` — monitor
- `canfar open <id>` — browser to notebook
- `canfar delete <id>` — teardown

**Auth / config**
- `canfar auth show` / `login cadc` — uses your `cadcproxy.pem`
- `canfar server ls` — pick CANFAR server
- `canfar stats` — cluster capacity

**Images you care about**
- `images.canfar.net/chimefrb-public/baseband-analysis:v1.9.0` — CHIME baseband pipeline (you already have one running: `n472h8x2`)
- `skaha/terminal:1.1.2` — generic shell for one-off commands

---

**Typical workflows for your project**

```bash
# See what's running
canfar ps

# Open existing CHIME notebook session
canfar open n472h8x2

# One-shot script on arc (no browser)
canfar create headless skaha/terminal:1.1.2 -- \
  python /arc/home/jfaber/baseband_morphologies/.../script.py

# Launch new CHIME analysis notebook
canfar create notebook images.canfar.net/chimefrb-public/baseband-analysis:v1.9.0
```

**Inside a session** you get native `/arc/...` paths — rename space/brace files `vos` can't touch, run `baseband_analysis`, inspect burst `.npy` where they live.

**What `canfar` cannot do:** replace `ssh h17`, edit local Mac files, or copy arc files (use `vcp` for that). It's session launcher + job runner, not SSH and not storage.

content.text:
**Yes** — request GPUs at session launch:

```bash
canfar create notebook skaha/base-notebook:latest --gpu 1
canfar create headless skaha/base-notebook:latest --gpu 1 -- python script.py
```

`-g` / `--gpu N` sets GPU count. Combine with fixed CPU/RAM if needed: `--cpu 4 --memory 16 --gpu 1`.

**Caveats:**
- **Image must support GPU** — needs CUDA-capable container (generic `skaha/terminal` may not; check image docs)
- **Availability not guaranteed** — `canfar stats` shows CPU/RAM only; GPU is scheduler-dependent (flexible mode may wait or fail)
- **Quota/limits** — your account may cap GPU count per session; `--dry-run` parses without launching

CHIME `baseband-analysis` image is CPU-focused for your pipeline; GPU would be for other images (ML, custom CUDA stacks). Check [CANFAR image registry](https://www.opencadc.org/canfar/) for GPU-tagged images.

+- Compute: `vos` is storage-only; use the `canfar` CLI for CANFAR Science Platform compute. Earlier we mistook storage-only `vos` access for "no CANFAR GPU access"; that is false. Verified 2026-06-25: `canfar create headless skaha/astroml-cuda:latest --gpu 1 -n gpu-smoke-test -- nvidia-smi` completed and logs showed `NVIDIA A100-PCIE-40GB` / CUDA 12.8 as a MIG slice (~20 GiB). Existing notebooks created without `--gpu` show `requestedGPUCores: "0"` and must be recreated with `--gpu N`.
 - Quota = 200 GB (`200000000000`). Was 99.75% full (`199496000484`, ~480 MiB free) before the 2026-06 cleanup.
*** End Patch

content.text:
`DATA_SOURCES.md` verified (**oracle**).

Live: session `q3yvurhw` Completed → `NVIDIA A100-PCIE-40GB`, `MIG 3g.20gb`; `n472h8x2` `requestedGPUCores=0`. Recorded `sha256=fe7300d894b1`.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>

and unlike hpcc, this A100 gpu is immeidately accessible?
</user_query>

Yes — in our tests CANFAR was effectively immediate; hpcc was not.

| | **CANFAR A100** | **hpcc v100** |
|---|---|---|
| Submit | accepted instantly | accepted instantly |
| Schedule | Pending → Completed in ~20–90s (3 smoke runs) | Pending `(Priority)` ~9+ min, never started |
| Use | `nvidia-smi` ran on A100 MIG slice | job cancelled while queued |

So for us: CANFAR GPU = fast/on-demand; hpcc GPU = permission OK, but queue wait (at least for v100 that night).

Caveats: CANFAR not guaranteed always instant (shared pool, quotas). hpcc varies by partition/GPU type/time — h100/h200 might differ from v100. Your running CANFAR notebook still has 0 GPUs until recreated with `--gpu N`.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>

where does a100 gpu fall in how powerful it is compared to the gpu's on hpcc?
</user_query>

CANFAR gave us **A100-PCIE-40GB**, but only a **MIG slice** (`3g.20gb`, ~20 GiB) — not the full card.

Rough hpcc ladder (single-GPU, ML-ish workloads):

| GPU (hpcc) | vs our CANFAR slice | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **P100** | much faster | old; hpcc has many |
| **V100** | clearly faster | hpcc workhorse; we queued forever on one |
| **A100 full** | ~2× our slice | same gen, but we get ~half SM + ~half mem |
| **H100** | ~2–3× A100 | hpcc `gpu` partition |
| **H200** | ~H100 + more HBM | hpcc `gpu` partition |
| **B300** | top tier | hpcc `vanvalen` (was down in our probe) |

**Bottom line:** our CANFAR GPU is **mid-high** — beats P100/V100, but below hpcc’s **H100/H200/B300**. And it’s a **shared MIG slice**, not a full A100.

For FLITS/baseband: plenty for interactive dev + moderate GPU work; hpcc H100/H200 wins for heavy batch fits if you can wait in queue.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>

Can we modify the MIG slice at all?
</user_query>

input.description: Dry-run request 2 GPUs

content.text:
No — not from our side.

`canfar` only exposes **`--gpu N`** (count). No MIG profile, no “full A100 vs 3g.20gb” knob. The **`3g.20gb` slice is chosen by CANFAR/skaha infra**, not by session config.

Inside the container you **can’t re-partition** the card (`nvidia-smi mig …` needs node admin). You just get whatever MIG instance was assigned.

**What you can do:**
- `--gpu 1` → ~20 GiB MIG slice (what we saw)
- `--gpu 2` → *might* mean 2 slices/GPUs, not a bigger single slice — untested live, may hit quota
- Need full 40GB A100 → ask CADC/CANFAR support or use hpcc H100/H200 when queue allows

CPU/RAM are tunable (`--cpu`, `--memory`); **GPU slice size is not**.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>

VERIFY GATE: substantive edits this turn have NOT been independently verified.

A clean compile / build / lint pass / "it ran" / your own confidence is NOT verification —
it shows the edit is well-formed, not that it is CORRECT. Before finishing you must run an
INDEPENDENT correctness check on each path and record it. Strongest first:
  - dispatch an independent reviewer subagent to try to BREAK the edit (adversarial-review)
  - a test that ASSERTS the right answer (test)
  - compare against a known-truth / reference / analytic value (oracle)
  - independently re-derive or reconstruct and check it agrees (reproduce / cross-check)
  - the user explicitly confirms (human)

Record it (this clears the gate):
  verify-gate record --paths <file> --method <adversarial-review|test|oracle|reproduce|cross-check|human> \
      --check "what you checked and how" --evidence "the concrete result: cmd+output / number / verdict"

Genuinely trivial edit (typo, comment, rename)? Log it, don't skip it silently:
  verify-gate record --paths <file> --method trivial --check "why trivial" --evidence "<what changed>"

input.contents:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Query machine_inventory.yaml for paths, hosts, and kinds."""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import sys
from pathlib import Path

try:
    import yaml
except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover
    raise SystemExit("PyYAML required: conda run -n py312 python -m pip install pyyaml") from exc

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
DEFAULT_INVENTORY = REPO_ROOT / "machine_inventory.yaml"


def load_inventory(path: Path) -> dict:
    with path.open(encoding="utf-8") as fh:
        return yaml.safe_load(fh)


def walk_locations(node: dict, machine: str, hits: list[dict]) -> None:
    loc = dict(node)
    loc.setdefault("machine", machine)
    hits.append(loc)
    for child in loc.get("children") or []:
        if isinstance(child, str):
            hits.append({"machine": machine, "path": child})
        elif isinstance(child, dict):
            walk_locations(child, machine, hits)


def flatten(inventory: dict) -> list[dict]:
    rows: list[dict] = []
    for machine, meta in (inventory.get("machines") or {}).items():
        for loc in meta.get("locations") or []:
            walk_locations(loc, machine, rows)
    return rows


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    parser.add_argument("--inventory", type=Path, default=DEFAULT_INVENTORY)
    parser.add_argument("--machine", help="Filter by machine id (iacobus, h17, arc, ...)")
    parser.add_argument("--kind", help="Filter by location kind")
    parser.add_argument("--path-contains", help="Substring match on path")
    parser.add_argument("--json", action="store_true", help="Emit JSON")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    inv = load_inventory(args.inventory)
    rows = flatten(inv)

    if args.machine:
        rows = [r for r in rows if r.get("machine") == args.machine]
    if args.kind:
        rows = [r for r in rows if r.get("kind") == args.kind]
    if args.path_contains:
        needle = args.path_contains.lower()
        rows = [r for r in rows if needle in str(r.get("path", "")).lower()]

    if args.json:
        json.dump(rows, sys.stdout, indent=2)
        sys.stdout.write("\n")
        return 0

    for row in rows:
        path = row.get("path", "?")
        kind = row.get("kind", "")
        size = row.get("size_human", "")
        extra = f" [{kind}]" if kind else ""
        size_s = f" {size}" if size else ""
        print(f"{row.get('machine')}:{path}{extra}{size_s}")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

# Machine inventory (CHIME–DSA codetections)

Canonical file: [`machine_inventory.yaml`](../../machine_inventory.yaml)

Live-probed 2026-06-25 across jakob-mbp, iacobus, h17, h23, hpcc, dsacamera, and arc (VOSpace).

## Quick reference

| Machine | Role | Key path |
|---------|------|----------|
| **iacobus** | data authority | `/Users/iacobus/Research/CHIME_DSA_Codetections` (218G) |
| **jakob-mbp** | dev + placeholders | `~/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS` |
| **arc** | institutional storage + CANFAR compute | `arc:home/jfaber/baseband_morphologies/chime_dsa_codetections` |
| **h17** | OVRO compute + arc archive | `/data/jfaber/arc_archive_2026-06`, `/data/research/.../chime-dsa-codetections` |
| **h23** | cold upstream | `/media/ubuntu/ssd/jfaber/chime_dsa_codetections` |
| **hpcc** | Slurm batch | `/home/jfaber/flits/dsa110-FLITS` |
| **dsacamera** | negligible codetection | — |

## Query with Python

```bash
# all paths on iacobus
python scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --machine iacobus

# every git repo entry
python scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --kind git_repo --json

# find DSA burst paths anywhere
python scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --path-contains DSA_bursts
```

## Query with yq

```bash
yq '.canonical' machine_inventory.yaml
yq '.machines.iacobus.locations[] | select(.id=="research_chime_codetections")' machine_inventory.yaml
yq '.. | select(has("path")) | .path' machine_inventory.yaml | rg burst
```

## Refresh

Re-run SSH/`vls` probes and update `generated_utc` + affected subtrees. Companion manifest for iacobus sentinels: [`codetections_manifest.yaml`](../../codetections_manifest.yaml).

content.text:
Live inventory built from SSH/`vls` probes (2026-06-25).

**Canonical file:** `dsa110-FLITS/machine_inventory.yaml`  
Full paths, sizes, file counts, git SHAs, GPU/compute notes for: jakob-mbp, iacobus, h17, h23, hpcc, dsacamera, arc.

**Query:**
```bash
python scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --machine iacobus
python scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --path-contains DSA_bursts
python scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --kind git_repo --json
yq '.canonical' machine_inventory.yaml
```

**Docs:** `docs/infrastructure/MACHINE_INVENTORY.md`  
**Pointer added:** `DATA_LOCATIONS.md` (Code section)

**Highlights:**
| Host | Key content |
|------|-------------|
| **iacobus** | 218G data authority — pickles (61G), archive (131G), npys (21G), waterfalls (6G) |
| **jakob-mbp** | FLITS @ `7e4c0c97`; local DSA_bursts 2.8G; iCloud placeholders only |
| **arc** | DSA_bursts ~2.9G; CHIME_bursts ~5.9G; OLD archive ~77.6G under baseband_morphologies |
| **h17** | arc_archive 36G; chime-dsa working tree 29G; 2× RTX 2080 Ti |
| **h23** | chime_dsa 8.8G; archive 86G; burstprop 42G |
| **hpcc** | FLITS @ `eed6f04`; 35 joint-fit JSONs |
| **dsacamera** | no codetection trees |

[REDACTED]

<user_query>

Develop a plan to migrate data/docs/files/code from all of these machines to just:
---
jakob-mbp, arc, h17, iacobus
</user_query>

Reviewing the inventory and consolidation docs to draft a migration plan scoped to jakob-mbp, arc, h17, and iacobus.

[REDACTED]

**Goal:** one logical project on **4 hosts only**. Retire h23 / hpcc / dsacamera as *homes* (not delete — quarantine + read-only).

---

## Role assignment (4-host model)

| Host | Owns | Does NOT own |
|------|------|--------------|
| **jakob-mbp** | Canonical **code** (`Developer/.../dsa110-FLITS`), Faber2026, small **local replicas** (≤5G), iCloud **placeholders** | Bulk data (25G free, 98% full) |
| **iacobus** | **Materialized data authority** (`~/Research/CHIME_DSA_Codetections`, 218G), iCloud uploader, Dropbox-Migration staging | Primary git dev (optional mirror only) |
| **arc** | **Institutional durable** burst `.npy`, baseband morphologies, CANFAR compute scratch (`flits_run`) | Interactive dev; not a git SoT |
| **h17** | **Compute + staging**: docker CHIME, upchan products, `chime-dsa-codetections` working tree, **h17-only** arc trash archive (`arc_archive_2026-06`, 36G) | Long-term sole copy of 218G iacobus tree |

**Git SoT:** GitHub `dsa110-FLITS` / `jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS` — not hpcc, not arc checkout, not h23.

---

## Phase 0 — Freeze & manifest (1 day)

1. Refresh [`machine_inventory.yaml`](machine_inventory.yaml) + [`codetections_manifest.yaml`](codetections_manifest.yaml) on iacobus.
2. Tag every path in inventory with **target host** + **action**: `keep | migrate | dedupe | quarantine | out_of_scope`.
3. Hard excludes: **nihari**, **Faber2024_bursts**, **h23 `/dataz/dsa110/T3` (59T)** — stay on h23 or separate policy; not part of 4-host migration.

**Exit:** manifest lists every source path → one of {jakob-mbp, iacobus, arc, h17, quarantine, leave_on_h23}.

---

## Phase 1 — Code (all machines → jakob-mbp + GitHub)

| Source | Action |
|--------|--------|
| jakob-mbp `dsa110-FLITS` @ `7e4c0c97` | **Keep** — canonical |
| hpcc `/home/jfaber/flits/dsa110-FLITS` @ `eed6f04` | Pull **unique** artifacts only (`analysis/scattering-refit-2026-06/_a1_fits/*.json`, 35 files) → commit or `iacobus:.../scattering_results/`; **retire** hpcc tree |
| arc `arc:home/jfaber/dsa110-FLITS` | Diff vs GitHub; rescue uncommitted CANFAR edits if any → PR; **stop using** arc as dev checkout |
| h17 `/data/research/.../chime-dsa-codetections` | **Not FLITS** — baseband/scripts; keep on h17 (Phase 3) |
| h23 `chime_dsa_codetections/` code | Drain notebooks/scripts worth keeping → FLITS repo; rest quarantine |
| dsacamera | **No action** — no codetection code found |
| Legacy Documents clones | Drain to `Developer/repos/...` + `_quarantine/`; update `machine_inventory` |

**Exit:** only jakob-mbp (+ GitHub) holds active FLITS; hpcc/dsacamera FLITS paths empty or quarantined.

---

## Phase 2 — Data tiering (what lives where)

**Rule:** each asset class has **one primary** + optional mirror.

| Asset class | Primary | Mirror / compute copy |
|-------------|---------|------------------------|
| 24 burst pickles (61G) | **iacobus** `burst_pickles/` | iCloud placeholder on jakob-mbp |
| DSA/CHIME `.npy` for fits | **arc** `.../data/DSA_bursts` + `CHIME_bursts` | jakob-mbp `dsa110-local-data/DSA_bursts` (2.8G, optional); iacobus if needed for offline |
| Full-Stokes waterfalls (6G) | **iacobus** | sourced from h23 (done) |
| `burst_npys` (21G) | **iacobus** | reconcile 32 vs 52 file count vs h23 before decommission |
| `scattering_results` | **iacobus** | merge hpcc JSON + h23 PDFs |
| `archive/` (131G on iacobus) | **iacobus** | arc `OLD_CHIME_DSA_Codetections` (77G) — **dedupe** then one canonical archive tier |
| `dm_budget`, `metadata`, `presentations` | **iacobus** | arc copies if newer — pick winner by mtime/hash |
| Baseband / singlebeam (14G on h17) | **h17** (compute) | promote finished products → **arc** or iacobus |
| `arc_archive_2026-06` (36G on h17) | **h17** backup of arc trash | optional copy → iacobus `archive/arc_trash_2026-06/` |
| CANFAR `flits_run` | **arc** (ephemeral) | don't migrate — regenerate |

**jakob-mbp disk:** do **not** materialize 218G locally; keep iCloud placeholders + 2.8G DSA replica max.

---

## Phase 3 — Source drain map

### h23 → iacobus / arc / quarantine

| h23 path | Size | Target | Status |
|----------|------|--------|--------|
| `.../chime_dsa_codetections/data/stokes_I_npys` | 983M | iacobus or arc | verify vs iacobus waterfalls |
| `.../chime_dsa_codetections/bursts` | 7.5G | iacobus `burst_npys` or arc | rsync + hash |
| `.../archive/OLD_CHIME_DSA_Codetections` | 79G | iacobus `archive/` | largely done; verify sentinel |
| `.../burstprop_paper/bursts` | 42G | iacobus `archive/burstprop_paper` | largely done |
| `.../archive/dsa110-scat` | 5.9G | git repo + iacobus if data | not duplicate in FLITS |
| `.../nihari` | 7.6G | **out of scope** | leave |
| `/dataz/dsa110/T3` | 59T | **leave on h23** | not in 4-host plan |

After verify: `h23:.../jfaber/` → `_quarantine/h23-drain-YYYYMMDD/` (mv, not rm).

### hpcc → jakob-mbp / iacobus

1. `rsync` `_a1_fits/*.json` → FLITS repo or iacobus.
2. Record Slurm job scripts worth keeping → `docs/infrastructure/hpcc/` in repo.
3. Quarantine `/home/jfaber/flits/` on hpcc.

### h17 — consolidate *on* h17 (already a target)

1. Keep `/data/research/.../chime-dsa-codetections` (29G) as **compute workspace**.
2. Keep `/data/jfaber/upchan_codetections` (473M).
3. Keep `/data/jfaber/arc_archive_2026-06` until iacobus archive dedupe done.
4. Remove empty stubs (`/data/ubuntu/chime-dsa-codetections`).

### arc — dedupe, don't bloat

1. **Do not** re-upload 218G from iacobus if arc already holds equivalents.
2. Run path-level diff: `DSA_bursts`, `CHIME_bursts` vs iacobus/jakob local.
3. Resolve arc `OLD_CHIME_DSA_Codetections` vs iacobus `archive/OLD_*` — one canonical, other becomes mirror or quarantined on arc after verify.
4. Quota watch: arc was ~64% after 2026-06 cleanup; large uploads need budget check.

### Dropbox / legacy Mac paths

- Dropbox codetection folders: already retired → ensure only **iacobus** holds bytes.
- `~/Documents/research/.../chime_dsa_codetections`: quarantine after git/data drain.
- `Archives/CHIME_canfar` on iacobus (2.7G): merge into iacobus archive or arc notebooks tier.

### dsacamera

- Mark **decommissioned** for codetections in `machine_inventory.yaml`; no migration.

---

## Phase 4 — Execution order

```
0. Manifest + disk budget (jakob-mbp, arc quota, iacobus 209G free)
1. Code: hpcc JSON + arc diff → git; retire hpcc FLITS
2. h23 delta rsync → iacobus (iacobus as uploader, not jakob-mbp)
3. arc ↔ iacobus dedupe (sentinels from codetections_manifest.yaml)
4. h17: no bulk move — only promote finished artifacts → arc/iacobus
5. Update DATA_LOCATIONS.md + machine_inventory.yaml (4-host only)
6. Quarantine sources on h23/hpcc/Documents with restore commands
7. Decommission checklist: no unique bytes left on retired hosts
```

**Transfers:** always **iacobus or h17 → arc/iacobus**; jakob-mbp only pulls ≤5G replicas. Use existing `h23_resilient_transfer.sh` pattern (iacobus pulls from h23).

---

## Phase 5 — Verification gates

Per subtree:
- file count + size match manifest
- SHA-256 sentinel from `codetections_manifest.yaml`
- FLITS `DATA_DIR` / configs point at **arc** paths or iacobus mirror
- `machine_inventory.yaml` query returns **zero** `keep` entries on h23/hpcc/dsacamera
- `_quarantine/README.md` entry with one-line restore

---

## Phase 6 — Ongoing 4-host ops

| Need | Where |
|------|-------|
| Edit FLITS | jakob-mbp → push GitHub |
| Bulk storage / iCloud | iacobus |
| Pipeline `.npy` / CANFAR fits | arc (+ `canfar --gpu`) |
| CHIME docker / upchan / 2×2080 Ti | h17 |
| Slurm big jobs | **Not** in 4-host set — hpcc optional burst compute only, no persistent data |

---

## Risks

- **jakob-mbp disk** — block any plan step that materializes iCloud tree locally.
- **arc quota (200G)** — dedupe before upload; iacobus is authority for 218G cold storage.
- **burst_npys 32 vs 52** — reconcile before h23 quarantine.
- **hpcc stale SHA** — treat as artifact source, not code branch.

---

input.contents:
# 4-host migration plan (CHIME–DSA codetections)

**Status:** planned (2026-06-25)  
**Inventory:** [`machine_inventory.yaml`](../../machine_inventory.yaml) (`migration_map`, per-path `migration_*` fields)  
**Sentinels:** [`codetections_manifest.yaml`](../../codetections_manifest.yaml)

Consolidate code, data, docs, and run artifacts onto **four hosts only**. Retire h23, hpcc, and dsacamera as *homes* (move-only quarantine — never delete).

## Target hosts

| Host | Role | Primary paths |
|------|------|---------------|
| **jakob-mbp** | Code + manuscripts + small local replicas | `~/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS` |
| **iacobus** | Materialized data authority + iCloud uploader | `~/Research/CHIME_DSA_Codetections` (218G) |
| **arc** | Institutional burst `.npy`, baseband, CANFAR compute | `arc:home/jfaber/baseband_morphologies/chime_dsa_codetections` |
| **h17** | OVRO compute, docker CHIME, staging archives | `/data/research/.../chime-dsa-codetections`, `/data/jfaber/arc_archive_2026-06` |

**Git source of truth:** GitHub `jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS` — not hpcc, arc checkout, or h23 trees.

## Retired hosts (drain → quarantine)

| Host | Action after drain |
|------|-------------------|
| **h23** | Read-only; unique bytes → iacobus/arc; then `mv` to `_quarantine/h23-drain-*` |
| **hpcc** | Pull run artifacts → git/iacobus; quarantine `/home/jfaber/flits/` |
| **dsacamera** | No codetection content; mark decommissioned in inventory |

**Out of scope:** nihari, Faber2024_bursts, h23 `/dataz/dsa110/T3` (59T raw pipeline).

## Data tiering

| Asset class | Primary | Mirror / compute |
|-------------|---------|------------------|
| FLITS code | jakob-mbp + GitHub | h17 optional clone for docker workflows |
| Burst pickles (61G) | iacobus | iCloud placeholders on jakob-mbp |
| DSA/CHIME `.npy` for fits | arc `DSA_bursts` / `CHIME_bursts` | jakob-mbp local replica ≤5G; iacobus optional |
| Waterfalls, npys, scattering | iacobus | merge hpcc JSON into scattering_results |
| Archive (131G+) | iacobus | dedupe vs arc `OLD_CHIME_DSA_Codetections` (77G) |
| Baseband / singlebeam | h17 compute workspace | promote finished products → arc |
| arc trash archive | h17 `arc_archive_2026-06` | optional copy → iacobus after dedupe |
| CANFAR `flits_run` | arc ephemeral | regenerate; do not migrate |

**jakob-mbp constraint:** 25G free (98% disk) — never materialize the 218G iacobus tree locally.

## Phases

### Phase 0 — Manifest (1 day)

1. Refresh `machine_inventory.yaml` and `codetections_manifest.yaml`.
2. Every path tagged in `migration_map` with `action`, `target_host`, `phase`, `status`.
3. Disk budget: jakob-mbp, arc quota (~200G), iacobus free space.

**Exit:** no unmapped codetection paths on retired hosts.

### Phase 1 — Code → jakob-mbp + GitHub

| Source | Action |
|--------|--------|
| jakob-mbp FLITS @ `7e4c0c97` | Keep canonical |
| hpcc `/home/jfaber/flits/dsa110-FLITS` | Pull `_a1_fits/*.json` (35) → repo or iacobus; quarantine hpcc tree |
| arc `arc:home/jfaber/dsa110-FLITS` | Diff vs GitHub; rescue CANFAR-only edits via PR; stop dev checkout |
| h23 `chime_dsa_codetections/` scripts | Worth keeping → FLITS; rest quarantine |
| Legacy Documents clones | Drain → `Developer/repos/` + `_quarantine/` |

### Phase 2 — Data drain (iacobus as uploader)

Use iacobus (not jakob-mbp) for h23→iacobus rsync (`h23_resilient_transfer.sh` pattern).

| h23 source | Target |
|------------|--------|
| `.../chime_dsa_codetections/bursts` (7.5G) | iacobus `burst_npys` (reconcile 32 vs 52 files first) |
| `.../data/stokes_I_npys` (983M) | iacobus waterfalls or arc |
| `.../archive/OLD_CHIME_DSA_Codetections` (79G) | iacobus `archive/` (verify sentinels) |
| `.../burstprop_paper/bursts` (42G) | iacobus `archive/burstprop_paper` |
| `.../archive/dsa110-scat` (5.9G) | git repo + iacobus if needed |

### Phase 3 — arc ↔ iacobus dedupe

1. Path-level diff: `DSA_bursts`, `CHIME_bursts` vs iacobus/jakob local.
2. Pick canonical for `OLD_CHIME_DSA_Codetections` (arc 77G vs iacobus 105G) by sentinel hash + mtime.
3. Do **not** bulk-upload 218G to arc without quota check.

### Phase 4 — h17 (already a target)

- Keep compute workspace (29G) and upchan products (473M).
- Promote finished artifacts → arc or iacobus only when stable.
- Remove empty stubs (`/data/ubuntu/chime-dsa-codetections`).

### Phase 5 — Quarantine + decommission

1. `_quarantine/README.md` entry per retired path with one-line restore.
2. Update `DATA_LOCATIONS.md` to 4-host model only.
3. Inventory query: zero `status: pending` on retired-host `migration_map` entries.

## Verification gates

Per subtree:

- File count + size match manifest
- SHA-256 sentinel from `codetections_manifest.yaml`
- FLITS configs point at arc paths or documented iacobus mirror
- `python scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --migration-status pending` returns empty

## Query migration state

```bash
# all pending migrations
python scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --migration-status pending

# everything targeting iacobus
python scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --migration-target iacobus

yq '.migration_map[] | select(.status=="pending")' machine_inventory.yaml
yq '.migration.target_hosts' machine_inventory.yaml
```

## Ongoing ops (post-migration)

| Need | Host |
|------|------|
| Edit FLITS | jakob-mbp → push GitHub |
| Bulk storage / iCloud | iacobus |
| Pipeline `.npy` / CANFAR GPU fits | arc |
| CHIME docker / upchan / 2×2080 Ti | h17 |
| Ad-hoc Slurm | hpcc burst only — no persistent project data |

## Risks

- jakob-mbp disk full — block local materialization of iCloud tree
- arc 200G quota — dedupe before upload
- burst_npys count mismatch (32 vs 52) — reconcile before h23 quarantine
- hpcc stale git SHA — artifact source only, not a branch

# Machine inventory (CHIME–DSA codetections)

Canonical file: [`machine_inventory.yaml`](../../machine_inventory.yaml)

Live-probed 2026-06-25 across jakob-mbp, iacobus, h17, h23, hpcc, dsacamera, and arc (VOSpace).

**4-host migration plan:** [`MIGRATION_PLAN_4HOST.md`](MIGRATION_PLAN_4HOST.md) — target hosts jakob-mbp, iacobus, arc, h17; retired h23, hpcc, dsacamera.

## Quick reference

| Machine | migration_status | Role | Key path |
|---------|------------------|------|----------|
| **iacobus** | target | data authority | `/Users/iacobus/Research/CHIME_DSA_Codetections` (218G) |
| **jakob-mbp** | target | dev + placeholders | `~/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS` |
| **arc** | target | institutional storage + CANFAR compute | `arc:home/jfaber/baseband_morphologies/chime_dsa_codetections` |
| **h17** | target | OVRO compute + arc archive | `/data/jfaber/arc_archive_2026-06`, `/data/research/.../chime-dsa-codetections` |
| **h23** | retired | cold upstream (drain) | `/media/ubuntu/ssd/jfaber/chime_dsa_codetections` |
| **hpcc** | retired | Slurm batch (drain) | `/home/jfaber/flits/dsa110-FLITS` |
| **dsacamera** | retired | negligible codetection | — |

## Query with Python

```bash
# all paths on iacobus
python scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --machine iacobus

# every git repo entry
python scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --kind git_repo --json

# find DSA burst paths anywhere
python scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --path-contains DSA_bursts

# pending migration_map entries
python scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --migration-map --migration-status pending

# locations on retired hosts
python scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --migration-status retired

# everything targeting iacobus
python scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --migration-target iacobus
```

## Query with yq

```bash
yq '.migration' machine_inventory.yaml
yq '.migration_map[] | select(.status=="pending")' machine_inventory.yaml
yq '.canonical' machine_inventory.yaml
yq '.machines.iacobus.locations[] | select(.id=="research_chime_codetections")' machine_inventory.yaml
yq '.. | select(has("path")) | .path' machine_inventory.yaml | rg burst
```

## Refresh

Re-run SSH/`vls` probes and update `generated_utc` + affected subtrees. Companion manifest for iacobus sentinels: [`codetections_manifest.yaml`](../../codetections_manifest.yaml).

INDEPENDENT correctness check on each path and record it. Strongest first:

cd /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS && python3 << 'PY'
import yaml, re, sys
from pathlib import Path

root = Path('.')
inv = yaml.safe_load((root / 'machine_inventory.yaml').read_text())
plan = (root / 'docs/infrastructure/MIGRATION_PLAN_4HOST.md').read_text()

errors = []

# 1. migration block consistency
m = inv.get('migration', {})
targets = set(m.get('target_hosts', []))
retired = set(m.get('retired_hosts', []))
if targets & retired:
    errors.append(f'overlap target/retired: {targets & retired}')
if len(targets) != 4:
    errors.append(f'expected 4 target_hosts, got {len(targets)}: {targets}')
if len(retired) != 3:
    errors.append(f'expected 3 retired_hosts, got {len(retired)}: {retired}')

# 2. every machine has migration_status in {target, retired}
for name, meta in inv.get('machines', {}).items():
    st = meta.get('migration_status')
    if st not in ('target', 'retired'):
        errors.append(f'{name}: missing/invalid migration_status={st!r}')
    elif st == 'target' and name not in targets:
        errors.append(f'{name}: migration_status=target but not in migration.target_hosts')
    elif st == 'retired' and name not in retired:
        errors.append(f'{name}: migration_status=retired but not in migration.retired_hosts')

# 3. migration_map entries reference valid hosts
all_hosts = targets | retired
for entry in inv.get('migration_map', []):
    for field in ('source_host', 'target_host'):
        h = entry.get(field)
        if h and h not in all_hosts and h != 'null':
            if h not in all_hosts:
                errors.append(f"migration_map {entry.get('id')}: {field}={h!r} not in machines")

# 4. plan doc linked in inventory
plan_doc = m.get('plan_doc')
if plan_doc and not (root / plan_doc).is_file():
    errors.append(f'plan_doc missing: {plan_doc}')

# 5. plan mentions all target hosts
for h in sorted(targets):
    if h not in plan and h.replace('-', '') not in plan.replace('-', ''):
        # jakob-mbp appears as jakob-mbp in table
        if h == 'jakob-mbp' and 'jakob-mbp' not in plan:
            errors.append(f'plan missing target host {h}')

# 6. count pending migration_map
pending = [e for e in inv.get('migration_map', []) if e.get('status') == 'pending']
print(f'OK: {len(inv["machines"])} machines, {len(inv["migration_map"])} migration_map entries, {len(pending)} pending')
if errors:
    print('FAILURES:')
    for e in errors:
        print(' ', e)
    sys.exit(1)
print('oracle cross-check: PASS')

input.command:
cd /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS && python3 << 'PY'
import subprocess, sys, json, yaml

def run(*args):
    return subprocess.run(
        ['python3', 'scripts/query_machine_inventory.py', *args],
        capture_output=True, text=True, check=True,
    )

inv = yaml.safe_load(open('machine_inventory.yaml'))
expected_pending = sum(1 for e in inv['migration_map'] if e.get('status') == 'pending')

pending = run('--migration-map', '--migration-status', 'pending')
lines = [l for l in pending.stdout.strip().splitlines() if l.strip()]
assert len(lines) == expected_pending, (len(lines), expected_pending)

retired = run('--migration-status', 'retired')
for h in inv['migration']['retired_hosts']:
    assert f'{h}:' in retired.stdout or h in retired.stdout, h

targets = inv['migration']['target_hosts']
for h in targets:
    out = run('--migration-target', h)
    assert out.stdout.strip(), f'no rows for target {h}'

j = run('--migration-map', '--migration-status', 'pending', '--json')
data = json.loads(j.stdout)
assert len(data) == expected_pending
assert all(d['status'] == 'pending' for d in data)

input.command:
cd /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS && python3 << 'PY'
import yaml, subprocess, sys, json
from pathlib import Path

inv = yaml.safe_load(open('machine_inventory.yaml'))
errors = []

targets = set(inv['migration']['target_hosts'])
retired = set(inv['migration']['retired_hosts'])
for name, meta in inv['machines'].items():
    st = meta.get('migration_status')
    if st == 'target' and name not in targets:
        errors.append(f'{name} target mismatch')
    if st == 'retired' and name not in retired:
        errors.append(f'{name} retired mismatch')

required = [
    'bursts', 'stokes_I_npys', 'dm_budget', 'scattering', 'dm', 'localizations'
]
sources = ' '.join(e.get('source_path','') for e in inv['migration_map'])
for r in required:
    if r not in sources:
        errors.append(f'h23 subtree {r} not in migration_map')

r = subprocess.run(['python3','scripts/query_machine_inventory.py','--check-retired-coverage'], capture_output=True, text=True)
if r.returncode != 0:
    errors.append(f'check-retired-coverage failed: {r.stdout} {r.stderr}')

out = subprocess.run(['python3','scripts/query_machine_inventory.py','--migration-target','iacobus'], capture_output=True, text=True, check=True)
assert 'h23_chime_bursts' in out.stdout, 'map rows missing from --migration-target union'
assert 'iacobus:' in out.stdout, 'location rows missing'

pending = sum(1 for e in inv['migration_map'] if e.get('status')=='pending')
assert pending == 19, f'expected 19 pending, got {pending}'

if errors:
    print('FAIL', errors); sys.exit(1)
print('oracle+test: PASS (19 pending, retired-coverage OK, target union OK)')

input.command:
cd /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS && python3 << 'PY'
import yaml, subprocess, sys
from pathlib import Path

inv = yaml.safe_load(open('machine_inventory.yaml'))
errors = []

# completed entries exist with completed_utc
for eid in ['hpcc_joint_fit_json', 'hpcc_flits_tree', 'dsacamera_decommission']:
    row = next((e for e in inv['migration_map'] if e['id']==eid), None)
    if not row or row.get('status') != 'completed':
        errors.append(f'{eid} not completed')
    if not row.get('completed_utc'):
        errors.append(f'{eid} missing completed_utc')

arc_run = next(e for e in inv['migration_map'] if e['id']=='arc_flits_run')
if arc_run.get('status') != 'skipped':
    errors.append('arc_flits_run not skipped')

pending = [e for e in inv['migration_map'] if e.get('status')=='pending']
if len(pending) != 15:
    errors.append(f'pending count {len(pending)} != 15')

# hpcc location points at quarantine
hpcc = inv['machines']['hpcc']
flits = next(l for l in hpcc['locations'] if l['id']=='flits_tree')
if 'quarantine/flits-20260625' not in flits['path']:
    errors.append('hpcc flits_tree path wrong')

# live hpcc
r = subprocess.run(['ssh','-o','BatchMode=yes','hpcc',
    'test -d /home/jfaber/_quarantine/flits-20260625 && ! test -e /home/jfaber/flits'],
    capture_output=True)
if r.returncode:
    errors.append('hpcc live quarantine check failed')

# local json count
local = Path('analysis/scattering-refit-2026-06/_a1_fits')
n = len(list(local.glob('*.json')))
if n != 22:
    errors.append(f'local json {n} != 22')

# log + plan files
for p in ['docs/infrastructure/MIGRATION_LOG.md','docs/infrastructure/MIGRATION_PLAN_4HOST.md']:
    if 'in progress' not in Path(p).read_text() and p.endswith('PLAN'):
        if 'in progress' not in Path(p).read_text():
            errors.append(f'{p} status line')

if 'hpcc_joint_fit_json' not in Path('docs/infrastructure/MIGRATION_LOG.md').read_text():
    errors.append('log missing hpcc entry')

r2 = subprocess.run(['python3','scripts/query_machine_inventory.py','--check-retired-coverage'], capture_output=True, text=True)
if r2.returncode:
    errors.append('retired-coverage failed')

if errors:
    print('FAIL:', errors); sys.exit(1)
print('oracle: PASS (3 completed, 1 skipped, 15 pending, hpcc quarantine live, 22 json local)')
PY

input.contents:
# Phase 2 design — h23 → iacobus data drain

**Status:** designed (2026-06-25)  
**Parent:** [`MIGRATION_PLAN_4HOST.md`](MIGRATION_PLAN_4HOST.md)  
**Inventory:** [`machine_inventory.yaml`](../../machine_inventory.yaml) (`migration_map` phase-2 entries)  
**Sentinels:** [`codetections_manifest.yaml`](../../codetections_manifest.yaml)

Phase 2 moves unique codetection bytes off **h23** into **iacobus** (`~/Research/CHIME_DSA_Codetections`). jakob-mbp never transits bulk data. After every subtree passes verification, h23 sources become read-only until Phase 5 quarantine.

---

## Executive summary

**Most Phase 2 targets may already be satisfied.** iacobus holds 218G materialized data (2026-06-17 manifest: 1546 files, sentinel-verified). April 2026 consolidation ran `h23_resilient_transfer.sh` from jakob-mbp. Phase 2 is therefore **audit-first, rsync-second**: measure delta per `migration_map` id, transfer only gaps, then re-run sentinels.

**Blockers before any rsync:**

1. **iacobus → h23 SSH** — `ssh iacobus 'ssh h23 …'` fails today (`Could not resolve hostname h23`). Fix iacobus `~/.ssh/config` (ProxyJump `dsa110maas` or Tailscale) before unattended pulls.
2. **Path corrections** — legacy script paths ≠ current h23 layout (see §Path corrections).
3. **burst_npys reconciliation** — iacobus 47 `.npy` / 32 top-level entries vs h23 `chime_dsa_codetections/bursts` 68 `.npy`; manifest still claims 52 from defunct `h23:…/burst_npys`.

**Estimated net new transfer (upper bound, if audit finds full delta):** ~55G (bursts 7.5G + stokes 983M + scat archive 5.9G + burstprop delta ~16G + archive delta TBD). Likely **≪55G** after dedupe.

---

## Architecture

```
jakob-mbp (orchestrator, logs)
    │  ssh -A iacobus
    ▼
iacobus ──rsync──► h23:/media/ubuntu/ssd/jfaber/…
    │
    └──► ~/Research/CHIME_DSA_Codetections/   (data authority)
              └── CloudDocs mirror (bird upload; not blocking Phase 2)
```

- **Orchestrator:** jakob-mbp runs `scripts/migration/h23_to_iacobus.sh` (wrapper around per-id jobs).
- **Data plane:** rsync executes **on iacobus**, pulling from h23 (`-e 'ssh …'`). Bytes never touch jakob-mbp disk.
- **Auth:** SSH agent forwarding jakob-mbp → iacobus → h23 (same pattern as legacy `h23_resilient_transfer.sh`).
- **Logs:** `~/logs/h23_transfers/` on jakob-mbp (or `$FLITS_ROOT/logs/migration/`).

---

## Path corrections (legacy script vs 2026-06 inventory)

| migration_map id | Legacy `h23_resilient_transfer.sh` | Correct h23 source (2026-06 probe) | iacobus target |
|------------------|-------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|----------------|
| `h23_stokes_i_npys` | `chime_dsa_codetections/data/` | `…/data/stokes_I_npys` (983M) | `dsa_fullstokes_waterfalls/` — **verify**: manifest source was `data/dsa_fullstokes_waterfalls`; may already be merged |
| `h23_chime_bursts` | `burst_npys/` (**path gone**) | `…/chime_dsa_codetections/bursts` (7.5G, 68 `.npy`) | `burst_npys/` |
| `h23_scattering` | `chime_dsa_codetections/scattering/` | same (21 files, 24M) | `scattering_results/` (115 files, 86M on iacobus — likely superset) |
| `h23_dm_budget` | `…/dm_budget/` → `dm_budget/h23_dm_budget/` | same | `dm_budget/h23_dm_budget/` |
| `h23_dm` | *(not in legacy script)* | `…/dm/` (732K) | merge into `dm_budget/` |
| `h23_localizations` | `…/localizations/` | same | `metadata/` |
| `h23_old_chime_archive` | `OLD_CHIME_DSA_Codetections/` (top-level) | `archive/OLD_CHIME_DSA_Codetections` (79G) | `archive/OLD_CHIME_DSA_Codetections/` (105G on iacobus — dedupe before rsync) |
| `h23_burstprop_bursts` | `burstprop_paper/` | `burstprop_paper/bursts` (42G) | `archive/burstprop_paper/` (26G on iacobus — delta ~16G) |
| `h23_dsa110_scat_archive` | *(not in legacy script)* | `archive/dsa110-scat` (5.9G) | `archive/dsa110-scat/` (new on iacobus) |

**Out of scope on h23:** `nihari/`, `/dataz/dsa110/T3`, `dsa110-continuum`, `tools/`, `ada/`, `ay122b` archives.

---

## Phase 2 sub-phases

### 2.0 — Prerequisites (half day)

| Step | Action | Exit |
|------|--------|------|
| 2.0a | Add h23 SSH stanza on **iacobus** (`Host h23`, `ProxyJump dsa110maas`, keys) | `ssh iacobus 'ssh h23 hostname'` → `lxd110h23` |
| 2.0b | Smoke rsync 1 MiB test file h23→iacobus | checksum match |
| 2.0c | Refresh `codetections_manifest.yaml` on iacobus (file counts + sentinels) | baseline for diff |

### 2.1 — Audit (per migration_map id)

Run from jakob-mbp:

```bash
python scripts/migration/audit_h23_delta.py --json reports/phase2_audit.json
```

For each phase-2 `migration_map` entry, record:

| Field | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `source_files` / `source_bytes` | h23 side |
| `target_files` / `target_bytes` | iacobus side |
| `overlap_files` | basename intersection |
| `missing_on_target` | need rsync |
| `extra_on_target` | keep; document |
| `sentinel_ok` | SHA-256 prefix vs `codetections_manifest.yaml` |
| `recommendation` | `skip` / `rsync_delta` / `reconcile` / `dedupe_first` |

**Expected audit outcomes (2026-06-25 probes):**

| id | Likely recommendation |
|----|------------------------|
| `h23_stokes_i_npys` | `skip` or `rsync_delta` — iacobus already 58 files / 6.0G waterfalls |
| `h23_scattering` | `skip` — iacobus superset |
| `h23_dm_budget`, `h23_dm`, `h23_localizations` | `skip` or tiny delta |
| `h23_chime_bursts` | **`reconcile`** — merge 68 h23 `.npy` into `burst_npys`; resolve naming (subdir vs flat) |
| `h23_old_chime_archive` | **`dedupe_first`** — iacobus 105G vs h23 79G; sentinel `freya_…pkl` |
| `h23_burstprop_bursts` | `rsync_delta` — ~16G gap |
| `h23_dsa110_scat_archive` | `rsync_delta` — 5.9G new |

### 2.2 — Transfer waves (smallest risk first)

Execute only ids where audit ≠ `skip`. Order:

| Wave | ids | ~size | Notes |
|------|-----|-------|-------|
| **A** | `h23_localizations`, `h23_dm`, `h23_scattering` | <25M | verify-only if audit says skip |
| **B** | `h23_dm_budget` | 329M | rsync into `dm_budget/h23_dm_budget/` |
| **C** | `h23_stokes_i_npys` | 983M | `--ignore-existing` if waterfalls overlap |
| **D** | `h23_chime_bursts` | 7.5G | **after** reconciliation policy (§burst_npys) |
| **E** | `h23_dsa110_scat_archive` | 5.9G | new tree under `archive/` |
| **F** | `h23_burstprop_bursts` | ≤16G delta | `--partial` resume |
| **G** | `h23_old_chime_archive` | 0–79G | **last**; dedupe policy required |

Each wave:

```bash
scripts/migration/h23_to_iacobus.sh --id h23_dm_budget --dry-run
scripts/migration/h23_to_iacobus.sh --id h23_dm_budget
python scripts/migration/audit_h23_delta.py --id h23_dm_budget  # post verify
```

Update `machine_inventory.yaml`: `status: completed`, `completed_utc`, `notes` with file/byte counts.

### 2.3 — burst_npys reconciliation policy

**Problem:** Three namespaces:

- h23 `chime_dsa_codetections/bursts/` — 68 `.npy`, burst-named subdirs
- iacobus `burst_npys/` — 47 `.npy`, 21G (partial vs manifest 52)
- manifest sentinel `burst_npys/I_240224aaad_mayra.npy` — may not exist on either side

**Policy (proposed):**

1. Inventory both trees → `reports/burst_npys_reconcile.csv` (path, size, sha256, burst nickname).
2. **Union** into iacobus `burst_npys/` with flat or `{nickname}/` layout — pick one; default **flat** to match existing iacobus layout.
3. Never delete h23 or iacobus files during reconcile; duplicates → `burst_npys/_dedupe_candidates/` on iacobus with manifest note.
4. Refresh `codetections_manifest.yaml` `burst_npys` sentinels from a file that exists post-merge.
5. Gate: `file_count ≥ 68` and sentinel pass before marking `h23_chime_bursts` completed.

### 2.4 — OLD_CHIME dedupe policy

iacobus `archive/OLD_CHIME_DSA_Codetections` (105G) vs h23 `archive/OLD_CHIME` (79G).

1. Compare sentinel `archive/OLD_CHIME_DSA_Codetections/polcal_fils/freya_230325aaag_fullstokes_interp.pkl` (SHA in manifest).
2. If sentinel passes on iacobus → **`skip` rsync**; mark completed with note "iacobus canonical".
3. If sentinel fails → path-level diff; rsync only `missing_on_target` from h23.
4. Phase 3 (`arc_old_chime_dedupe`) handles arc 77G — do not conflate with Phase 2.

---

## Verification gates (per id)

1. **Post-rsync:** `audit_h23_delta.py --id …` → `missing_on_target: 0`
2. **Sentinel:** first 64 MiB SHA-256 matches `codetections_manifest.yaml` (or updated sentinel after reconcile)
3. **Inventory:** `migration_map[].status: completed`
4. **Global:** `python scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --migration-map --migration-status pending | rg '^h23_'` → empty

---

## Scripts to add (Phase 2 implementation — not yet written)

| Script | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `scripts/migration/h23_to_iacobus.sh` | Per-id rsync wrapper; reads paths from `machine_inventory.yaml` via yq |

    errors.append('h23_chime_bursts source_path wrong')

# design doc exists + sections
p2 = (root/'docs/infrastructure/PHASE2_DESIGN.md').read_text()
for needle in ['2.0', 'audit_h23_delta.py', 'h23_to_iacobus.sh', 'burst_npys', 'iacobus → h23 SSH']:
    if needle not in p2:
        errors.append(f'PHASE2 missing {needle!r}')

plan = (root/'docs/infrastructure/MIGRATION_PLAN_4HOST.md').read_text()
if 'PHASE2_DESIGN.md' not in plan:
    errors.append('MIGRATION_PLAN missing PHASE2 link')

log = (root/'docs/infrastructure/MIGRATION_LOG.md').read_text()

mi = (root/'docs/infrastructure/MACHINE_INVENTORY.md').read_text()
if 'PHASE2_DESIGN.md' not in mi:
    errors.append('MACHINE_INVENTORY missing PHASE2 link')

# live oracle: design claims vs probe
r = subprocess.run(['ssh','-o','BatchMode=yes','h23',
    'test ! -d /media/ubuntu/ssd/jfaber/burst_npys; find /media/ubuntu/ssd/jfaber/chime_dsa_codetections/bursts -name "*.npy" | wc -l'],
    capture_output=True, text=True)
if r.returncode != 0:

else:

r2 = subprocess.run(['ssh','-o','BatchMode=yes','iacobus',

if r2.returncode == 0:

r3 = subprocess.run(['ssh','-o','BatchMode=yes','iacobus','ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=10 h23 echo ok'],
    capture_output=True, text=True)
if r3.returncode == 0:
    errors.append('iacobus->h23 unexpectedly works; design says blocked')
elif 'Could not resolve hostname h23' not in r3.stderr and 'Could not resolve hostname h23' not in r3.stdout:

if errors:
    print('FAIL:', errors); sys.exit(1)
print(f'oracle PASS: h23 68 npy, iacobus waterfalls 58, burst_npys note ok, links ok, iacobus->h23 blocked')

input.contents:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Phase 2: rsync one migration_map id from h23 into iacobus (runs rsync ON iacobus).
set -euo pipefail

REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." && pwd)"
INVENTORY="$REPO_ROOT/machine_inventory.yaml"
IACOBUS="${IACOBUS_HOST:-iacobus}"
LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-$HOME/logs/h23_transfers}"
RSYNC_OPTS='-av --partial --ignore-existing --stats'
H23_SSH='ssh -o ServerAliveInterval=30 -o ServerAliveCountMax=6 -o ConnectTimeout=60'

usage() {
  echo "Usage: $0 --id MIGRATION_MAP_ID [--dry-run]" >&2
  exit 1
}

DRY=0
ID=""
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
  case "$1" in
    --id) ID="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --dry-run) DRY=1; shift ;;
    *) usage ;;
  esac
done
[[ -n "$ID" ]] || usage

mapfile -t FIELDS < <(python3 - "$INVENTORY" "$ID" <<'PY'
import sys, yaml
inv = yaml.safe_load(open(sys.argv[1]))
e = next(x for x in inv["migration_map"] if x["id"] == sys.argv[2])
src = e["source_path"]
dst = e["target_path"]
if e["id"] == "h23_dm_budget":
    dst = dst.rstrip("/") + "/h23_dm_budget"
print(src)
print(dst)
PY
)
SRC="${FIELDS[0]}"
DST="${FIELDS[1]}"
NAME="$ID"
LOG="$LOG_DIR/${NAME}.log"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"

H23_SRC="h23:${SRC}/"
# trailing slash on dst dir
IACOBUS_DST="$DST/"

{
  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] START $NAME dry_run=$DRY"
  echo "  src: $H23_SRC"
  echo "  dst: $IACOBUS_DST"
} >>"$LOG"

RSYNC_DRY=()
(( DRY )) && RSYNC_DRY=(--dry-run)

# rsync executes on iacobus; jakob-mbp forwards agent for h23 auth chain
if ssh -A -o BatchMode=yes "$IACOBUS" \
  "mkdir -p '$IACOBUS_DST' && rsync ${RSYNC_DRY[*]:-} $RSYNC_OPTS -e '$H23_SSH' '$H23_SRC' '$IACOBUS_DST'" >>"$LOG" 2>&1; then
  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] SUCCESS $NAME" >>"$LOG"
  echo "OK $NAME (log: $LOG)"
else
  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] FAILED $NAME" >>"$LOG"
  echo "FAIL $NAME — see $LOG" >&2
  exit 1
fi

content.text:
<user_query>

can i ask the reason for b2/s3 bucket over google drive?
</user_query>

**Short answer:** Google Drive *can* work; B2/S3 was ranked higher for a **280 GiB canonical authority** you pull from **any host** (especially **h17/Linux**), not because GDrive is unusable.

**Why B2/S3 over Google Drive here**

| Factor | B2 / S3 | Google Drive (your Caltech mount) |
|--------|---------|----------------------------------|
| **Linux / h17** | Native via `rclone` + S3 API; no desktop client | Needs `rclone drive` or browser; no verified setup on h17 |
| **280 GiB bulk tree** | Built for object storage; sync/copy is the normal workflow | Works, but Drive API quotas, rate limits, and “many small files” pain are common at this scale |
| **“Authority” model** | One bucket URI → any machine pulls the same tree | Fine if you treat it as canonical and sync deliberately; today **no codetection folder exists** on your mount |
| **Quota certainty** | Pay-as-you-go (~$2/mo for ~300 GiB) | **Unknown** — Workspace/edu quota not probed; could block a one-shot 280 GiB upload |
| **Compute pattern** | Pull subset to `/data/`, leave cold data in cloud | Same possible, but less standard for HPC-style pipelines |
| **Institutional fit** | Personal/ project bucket you control | Caltech account — good if quota is generous and policy allows research bulk storage |

**When GDrive is the better pick**

- Caltech gives you **enough quota** (check before committing).
- You’re OK making Drive the **single canonical path** and using `rclone` (not Finder sync) from Mac + h17 + hpcc.
- You want **$0 marginal cost** and don’t need S3-style tooling elsewhere.

**Why the audit didn’t lead with GDrive:** it wasn’t set up for this dataset, quota wasn’t verified, and your stated ideal (“fully in cloud, easily downloadable to **any** machine”) maps cleanly to object storage + `rclone` on every host. GDrive is a **viable second** once quota and a canonical `Research/CHIME_DSA_Codetections` layout are confirmed.

Want a quick probe of Caltech Drive free space and whether `rclone` can see it from jakob-mbp?

content.text:
**Partly.** AI Pro fixes the **capacity** objection; several **technical** ones stay.

**What changes with Google AI Pro (5 TB bundled, ~$20/mo)**  
- ~280 GiB fits easily — no “quota unknown” blocker.  
- If you already pay for AI Pro, storage is **sunk cost** vs ~$2/mo B2.  
- GDrive is a **serious** canonical option on your **personal** Google account.

**What still applies**

| Issue | Still true? |
|--------|-------------|
| **Account split** | Audit checked **Caltech** Drive mount — empty. AI Pro storage is usually **personal** (@gmail). Pick one account as authority; don’t mix. |
| **h17 / Linux** | No Drive desktop app — still **`rclone`** (OAuth to personal account). Works; must set up once on h17. |
| **280 GiB bulk upload** | Drive API rate limits / many-file trees — slower and fussier than S3, but doable with `rclone copy` + retries. |
| **jakob-mbp disk (19 GiB free)** | Finder sync still a bad idea — upload **iacobus → Drive via rclone**, not via Mac cache. |
| **5 TB is shared** | Drive + Gmail + Photos — leave headroom if Photos/Gmail are heavy. |
| **hpcc / campus** | Same as h17: rclone from personal account; campus VPN OK. |

**Bottom line:** With AI Pro, GDrive is **viable canonical storage** if you commit to **personal Google One Drive + rclone everywhere**. B2/S3 still wins on **bulk/HPC ergonomics** (S3 API, fewer API quirks, no shared 5 TB bucket with email/photos). CANFAR stays the institutional/compute tier either way.

**Practical fork:** GDrive authority = cheap (already subscribed), one Google login, acceptable rclone pain. B2 authority = ~$2/mo extra, cleaner multi-host pipeline, keeps research data off personal Google quota.

<user_query>

how can I setup rclone in the easiest possible way?
</user_query>

**Easiest path for your case** (`gdrive-jakob` → `jakobtfaber@gmail.com`, then iacobus):

### 1. jakob-mbp (one interactive pass)

```bash
rclone config
```

| Prompt | Answer |
|--------|--------|
| `n` / name | `gdrive-jakob` |
| Storage | `drive` (Google Drive) |
| client_id / secret | Enter (blank = rclone defaults) |
| scope | `1` — full Drive access |
| service_account | Enter (blank) |
| auto config | `y` → browser opens → sign in **jakobtfaber@gmail.com** |
| Shared drive | `n` |
| advanced | `n` |
| confirm | `y` |

Verify:

```bash
rclone about gdrive-jakob:
rclone mkdir gdrive-jakob:Research/CHIME_DSA_Codetections
```

That’s it on the Mac. No Google Cloud console project unless you hit OAuth app limits (unlikely for personal use).

---

### 2. iacobus (headless — paste token, don’t re-browser)

On **jakob-mbp**:

```bash
rclone authorize "drive"
```

Copy the **whole JSON blob** it prints.

On **iacobus**:

```bash
rclone config
# n → gdrive-jakob → drive → blank client_id/secret
# auto config → n
# config_token → paste JSON
# rest → defaults
```

Verify:

```bash
ssh iacobus 'rclone about gdrive-jakob:'
```

---

### 3. Smoke test (from FLITS worktree)

```bash
cd ~/Developer/scratch/worktrees/flits-gdrive-authority
scripts/migration/iacobus_to_gdrive.sh --subdir metadata
```

---

**Even lazier on iacobus:** after step 1 works on jakob-mbp, copy the remote block:

```bash
scp ~/.config/rclone/rclone.conf iacobus:~/.config/rclone/rclone.conf
# merge if iacobus already has dropbox/iclouddrive remotes
```

Often works; token paste is the “official” headless way.

**Skip:** Drive for Desktop — not needed for rclone. **Don’t** route 283 GiB through jakob-mbp disk; upload stays iacobus → Drive once both hosts have `gdrive-jakob`.

That field is only for **Google Workspace Shared Drives** (team drives). You're using personal **My Drive** on `jakobtfaber@gmail.com`, so:

- Interactive prompt **“Shared drive?”** → **`n`**
- Advanced **`team_drive`** → **don’t set it** / leave empty

Files go to `gdrive-jakob:Research/CHIME_DSA_Codetections/` on your personal Drive, not a shared drive. Only fill it if you deliberately want a specific Shared Drive ID (you’d pick that drive in the rclone shared-drive list).

<user_query>

is there any alternative?
</user_query>

**Realistic alternatives** (288 GiB → cloud authority):

| Path | ETA (order of magnitude) | Catch |
|------|--------------------------|--------|
| **Keep current** (iacobus 4× parallel → GDrive) | ~2–3 days | Already running; uplink ~6–7 Mbps |
| **Priority-only to GDrive** | ~8 h for ~35 GiB | Skip `archive/` (193 GiB, mostly OLD) + `burst_pickles/` (61 GiB); fits first |
| **LAN → jakob-mbp → GDrive** | ~3–4 h upload | Visit iacobus, rsync over ethernet; MBP **252 Mbps up**. Needs local disk or selective rsync — not Tailscale tonight |
| **External SSD shuttle** | Same day once copied | Copy on iacobus, plug into MBP, `rclone copy` |
| **CANFAR quota bump** | Days (support ticket) | Institutional; ~113 GiB already on arc; need **~350 GiB** quota; weak “any machine” vs rclone |
| **hpcc `/resnick/groups/radiolab`** | Campus store, not cloud | 15 TiB free; still **iacobus→hpcc** at ~6 Mbps (~3 days); GDrive from hpcc blocked (~87 B/s to Google in prior probe) |
| **B2/S3 instead of GDrive** | Same as iacobus upload | Different bucket, **same home uplink** |
| **GCP VM “near Google”** | No win | Data still enters via iacobus uplink first |

**Only paths that beat ~2 days materially:**
1. **Get bits onto jakob-mbp** (LAN visit, SSD, or fix Tailscale + selective rsync) → GDrive in **hours**.
2. **Don’t upload everything yet** — GDrive authority for **active** trees (`burst_npys`, scattering, metadata, dm_budget); leave **OLD archive** on iacobus/hpcc until a faster pipe exists.

**Honest:** no magic cloud switch fixes **iacobus upload**. Faster = **different machine on a fat pipe** or **smaller canonical set**.
